strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq
Founded and maintains an event-driven open-source platform demonstrating long-term ownership
concerns: No evidence of executive leadership (CTO/VP) or scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ people
No demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems experience — only recent, vague 'contribution' to agent infrastructure
No fintech credit-risk, working-capital, or regulated financial-systems experience; RIDE Capital tenure was as an individual contributor

key_factors.factor: Lacks production multi-agent AI systems experience
No fintech credit/risk or regulated-environment depth

response.justification: The candidate's profile is that of a senior individual contributor and software craftsman, not an executive technologist. The resume shows no CTO, VP Engineering, or Head-of-Engineering tenure, no evidence of scaling teams from 10 to 30+, no board-level communication experience, and no governance of hybrid/outsourced delivery models — all hard requirements. Agentic AI exposure is limited to recent, loosely described 'contributions' rather than architecting and operating production multi-agent systems with feedback loops, evaluation pipelines, and observability. Fintech experience is thin: a 15-month stint at RIDE Capital as a developer doing TDD does not equate to credit-risk, liquidity, or regulated-compliance architecture. The fit for an executive agentic-AI fintech role is weak.
